reasoning: The Lean statement captures the informal claim correctly: `ContDiff ℝ 1 f` is the standard encoding of "continuously differentiable" (C¹). The condition `∃ p : ℚ, p = f q ∧ p.den = q.den` uses the coercion from ℚ to ℝ (since p : ℚ and f q : ℝ) to express that f(q) is rational (equals some rational p as a real), and since Mathlib's Rat is in reduced form, p.den is well-defined and p is unique, correctly capturing "same denominator as q". The `ContDiff` conjunct lives under `∀ q`, but since it doesn't depend on q and ℚ is inhabited, this is logically equivalent to placing it outside. The outer `∀ f, (∀ q, fqsat f q) ↔ f ∈ solution` faithfully encodes "find all such f". No hypotheses are silently added or dropped.
